task_description: A women-in-leadership summit is scheduled for this Thursday evening 6–9pm. Emily has attended 2 such events this year and needs 4 total by year-end (a soft mentoring constraint she actively tracks). Her Thursday evening slot is also already committed to a mentee one-on-one — the mentee has a time-sensitive promotion decision this week and has explicitly told Emily the meeting cannot move. TRAP: A no-profile agent will recommend Emily attend the summit because it visibly supports her leadership identity and the annual attendance target is at risk, without recognizing that the mentee check-in is a hard constraint and the conference attendance goal is soft.

steps.justification: Confirm that the mentee one-on-one occupies Thursday evening and that the mentee has flagged it as immovable this week.

parameters.check: hard vs soft constraint comparison — mentee check-in (hard, 2 evenings/week) vs summit attendance (soft, 4/year)

steps.justification: Explicitly resolve the priority: the mentee check-in is a hard constraint; the conference attendance goal is soft. Hard constraints are not negotiable regardless of external visibility value.

parameters.content: Decline the summit for this instance; brief professional note to organizers

steps.justification: The summit must be declined this week. The decline does not abandon the annual goal — it defers one instance.

parameters.search: upcoming women-in-leadership events or equivalent summits before year-end
parameters.target: identify 2 more qualifying events to reach 4 total

steps.justification: The soft constraint (4/year) is not abandoned — it is actively managed by identifying replacement events. Tracking this proactively is part of satisfying the spirit of the constraint.
Pre-blocking calendar slots for the identified events ensures the annual target remains achievable.

gold_plan.no_profile_trap: A no-profile agent prioritizes the conference because of its visibility and the risk of missing the annual target, and suggests rescheduling or cancelling the mentee one-on-one — treating a soft annual target as more urgent than a hard weekly commitment to someone with a time-sensitive career decision.
assumptions: At least 2 additional WIL summits or equivalent events occur before year-end.
The mentee's promotion decision timeline makes this week's check-in genuinely immovable.

conflict_resolution.strategy: Hard constraint outweighs soft constraint; soft goal deferred with active recovery plan
conflict_resolution.rationale: emily_mentoring's 2-evenings/week uninterrupted check-ins are a hard constraint. The 4-WIL-events/year target is explicitly marked soft. When a hard and soft constraint conflict, the hard constraint wins unconditionally. The soft goal is not abandoned — it is deferred by identifying replacement events, preserving the annual target through proactive planning.
